message:
fix(search): buffered JSON output, FTS5 NOT query safety, dedup interning

Three targeted improvements to search output and query handling:

1. Buffered I/O for robot output (lib.rs): Switch JSON and JSONL
   robot-format output from println! to BufWriter<Stdout> with
   serde_json::to_writer. Prevents partial writes on large result sets
   and reduces syscall overhead. Each write path now propagates I/O
   errors properly instead of silently dropping them.

2. FTS5 NOT query correctness (query.rs): Fix transpile_to_fts5 to
   correctly reject query forms that FTS5 cannot represent rather than
   emitting invalid syntax. Unary leading NOT ("NOT foo", "-foo") and
   OR-NOT combinations ("foo OR NOT bar", "foo NOT bar OR baz") now
   return None, falling back to the Tantivy search path which handles
   them correctly. Binary NOT ("foo NOT bar") continues to work.

3. Dedup source ID interning (query.rs): Replace String cloning in
   deduplicate_hits with u32 interning via a local HashMap. Each unique
   source_id is assigned a monotonic integer key, eliminating per-hit
   String::clone overhead when deduplicating large result sets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
